steps.title: Identify the function's input and output variables
steps.content: In the function $K(v) = \frac{1}{2}(41)(v^2)$, the input variable is $v$ (speed in meters per second) and the output is $K$ (kinetic energy in joules). The notation $K(0)$ means we evaluate $K$ when $v = 0$.

steps.title: Interpret K(0) = 0 in context
steps.content: $$K(0) = \frac{1}{2}(41)(0^2) = 0$$This shows that when speed equals 0 meters per second, kinetic energy equals 0 joules. That matches choice C — C is correct.
